title: Vectors — a list of numbers representing multiple properties at once
verdict: PASS — all 8 check groups clear. Reading is 791 words (range 600–900), 3 sections-correct headings in order, all 3 citation markers used inline and in bibliography with primary [1] cited 10+ times, no forward references (similarity deferred to 6.6, embeddings deferred to later module, matrices not mentioned), all 10 sampled assertions trace to corpus, beginner readability strong (concrete-first, bold-term definitions, no jargon dumps), no assessment references, no scope creep.

format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is H1 title. Sidecar valid. Exactly 6 required sections in correct order.

length.notes: 791 words is within the 600–900 standard range. 791/230 = 3.44 min.

citations.notes: All 3 markers cited inline. Bibliography entries match. Primary [1] appears at lines 5, 11, 13, 29, 41, 46, 71, 102, 119, 121, 127.

no_forward_references.notes: Matrices not mentioned. Dot product and embeddings each receive exactly one name-and-defer sentence, not taught.

faithfulness.notes: All 10 sampled assertions trace directly to corpus sections 5.1–5.5, 6, and 7. Phrasing is paraphrased or closely matched.

beginner_readability.notes: vector, dimension, feature, feature vector all defined at first use with plain-language meaning. scalar back-referenced to prior topic 6.2. Concrete-first: song analogy in Overview before abstraction. No hedging phrases. Average sentence length well under 25 words.

formatting.notes: No paragraph exceeds 6 sentences. Worked Example is a numbered list. In Practice Do/Don't uses bullet lists. Inline code blocks and a table appear in Key Concepts. Bold-term/plain-meaning shape used for all defined terms.

assessment_references.notes: No mention of A2, due week 6, 15%, or any assessment ID or weight in the reading.

scope.notes: Reading stays within the headline: vectors as ordered lists of scalars representing multiple properties. Similarity calculation deferred. Embeddings deferred. Matrices not introduced. No content from 6.4, 6.5, or 6.6 is taught.
